q.en: What is Carlos especially good at?

explain.en: The book says Carlos 'was an expert at finding the path from beginning to end without getting lost.'
explain.zh: 书中说卡洛斯“是位专家，能不迷路地从起点找到终点”。

q.en: What was the penalty for the brother who finished last in the maze contest?
q.zh: 迷宫比赛中最后完成的人要受到什么惩罚？

options.en: He had to do all the chores in the house for a month
options.zh: 他必须包揽家里一个月的全部家务

options.en: He had to cook dinner according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他必须做晚饭（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: He had to run a mile

explain.en: Javier suggests 'the last one through the maze does all the chores in the house for a month.'
explain.zh: 哈维尔提议“最后走出迷宫的人包揽家里一个月的全部家务”。

q.en: According to Carlos, how is a corn maze made?
q.zh: 根据卡洛斯的说法，玉米迷宫是怎么做成的？

options.en: By cutting paths through stalks in cornfields
options.zh: 在玉米地里开辟通道，把玉米秆割开

options.en: By painting lines on the ground according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 在地面上画线（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Carlos explains corn mazes are '3-D and made by cutting paths through stalks in cornfields.'
explain.zh: 卡洛斯解释，玉米迷宫是“立体的，通过在玉米地里开辟通道做成”。

q.en: What picture was the corn maze designed to show when seen from above?
q.zh: 从上方俯瞰，这个玉米迷宫被设计成什么图案？

options.en: A map of the farm according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 农场的地图（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: An apple tree full of fruit
options.zh: 一棵结满果实的苹果树

explain.en: Carlos knew 'the maze was created to make a picture of an apple tree full of fruit.'
explain.zh: 卡洛斯知道“迷宫被设计成从空中看像一棵结满果实的苹果树”。

q.en: Why did Carlos believe he could beat Javier even though Javier is faster?
q.zh: 既然哈维尔跑得更快，卡洛斯为什么相信自己能赢？

options.en: Carlos is smarter and uses strategy, while Javier just races
options.zh: 卡洛斯更聪明、讲究策略，而哈维尔只是横冲直撞

options.en: The dog helped Carlos according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 狗帮了卡洛斯（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Carlos says 'He's faster, but I'm smarter' and plans to 'take our time and think about each of the paths.'
explain.zh: 卡洛斯说“他更快，但我更聪明”，并打算“慢慢来，把每条路都想一想”。

q.en: What happened to Javier inside the maze?
q.zh: 哈维尔在迷宫里发生了什么事？

options.en: He got lost forever according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他永远迷路了（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: He twisted his ankle badly in a hollow in the dirt path
options.zh: 他在土路的一个坑里严重崴了脚踝

explain.en: The book says Javier 'had twisted his ankle badly in a hollow worn in the dirt path.'
explain.zh: 书中说哈维尔“在土路被踩出的坑里严重崴伤了脚踝”。

q.en: What 'dilemma' did Carlos face near the exit?
q.zh: 在出口附近，卡洛斯面临什么“两难”？

options.en: Whether to eat or keep walking according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 要不要吃东西还是继续走（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Whether to win the contest or go back to help his hurt brother
options.zh: 是去赢比赛，还是回头去帮助受伤的弟弟

explain.en: Carlos's dilemma was to 'take the final steps to the exit and win' or 'go back to help him.'
explain.zh: 卡洛斯的难题是“走向出口赢下比赛”还是“回头去帮他”。

q.en: About how long did it take Carlos to solve the maze before he heard Javier calling?
q.zh: 在听到哈维尔呼救前，卡洛斯大约花了多久解开迷宫？

options.en: About thirty seconds according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 大约三十秒（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says about ten minutes to get close, then 'about another five minutes' to the exit (around 15 total).
explain.zh: 书中说他大约十分钟就接近出口，又“大约五分钟”到达出口（共约15分钟）。

q.en: Why did the author include Javier's 'flash of pride' at the end?
q.zh: 作者为什么在结尾写哈维尔眼中“闪过一丝自豪”？

options.en: To show the maze was easy according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了表现迷宫很简单（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）
为了表现哈维尔在生气

options.en: To show that helping family mattered more to Carlos than winning
options.zh: 为了表现对卡洛斯来说，帮助家人比赢更重要

explain.en: The book says seeing Javier's pride 'was better than winning,' showing the value of family over victory.
explain.zh: 书中说看到哈维尔的自豪“比赢更美好”，体现家人之情重于胜负。

q.en: What did Carlos do when he heard Javier calling for help?
q.zh: 听到哈维尔呼救时，卡洛斯做了什么？

options.en: He retraced his steps and went back to help his brother
options.zh: 他原路返回，去帮助弟弟

options.en: He ran straight to the exit
He ignored it and finished the maze according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他置之不理，走完了迷宫（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says Carlos 'retraced his steps' and went back after hearing Javier say 'this is no joke!'
explain.zh: 书中说卡洛斯听到哈维尔说“这不是开玩笑！”后“原路返回”去帮他。

prompt.en: Carlos was about to win when he chose to help Javier instead. Have you ever given up something you wanted to help someone else? Describe the choice and how it felt, like Carlos at the end of the story.
prompt.zh: 卡洛斯眼看要赢了，却选择去帮助哈维尔。你有没有曾经放弃自己想要的东西去帮助别人？像故事结尾的卡洛斯那样，讲讲你的选择和当时的感受。

en: Describe the situation and what you gave up.
Explain how you felt, comparing it to Carlos feeling pride was 'better than winning.'
zh: 描述当时的情形，以及你放弃了什么。
说明你的感受，并与卡洛斯觉得自豪“比赢更美好”作比较。
